visuals.title: Memory Segmentation: Address Translation
visuals.description: Shows how the CPU generates a logical address [s|d], indexes the segment table to retrieve base and limit, validates the offset against the limit, and computes the physical address as base + d.

diagrams.title: Segmentation Address Translation Flow
steps: Process is divided into variable-sized segments (code, stack, heap, data)
CPU generates logical address [segment number s | offset d]
Segment number s indexes the Segment Table
Segment Table entry provides base address and limit for segment s
Hardware checks: is offset d < limit?
If d >= limit, trap is generated (segmentation fault)
If d < limit, physical address = base + d
Access physical memory at computed address
diagrams.notes: Unlike paging, segments are variable-sized and correspond to logical divisions of a program. External fragmentation is a drawback of segmentation.

diagrams.title: Segment Table Structure
diagrams.notes: Each segment table entry maps a segment number to its physical base address and length (limit). The STBR (Segment Table Base Register) points to the segment table in memory.
